breaking_news.title: Handala Hacktivists Wipe 200,000 Stryker Devices Without Malware
breaking_news.summary:
Medical technology giant Stryker confirmed a cyberattack that remotely wiped tens of thousands of employee devices across 79 countries without using traditional malware. The pro-Palestinian hacktivist group Handala claimed responsibility, stating they exfiltrated approximately 50TB of corporate data and disabled electronic ordering systems.

While Stryker assured that medical devices remain safe for patient use, the disruption to internal Microsoft environments has forced customers to place orders manually. This incident highlights the growing risk of hacktivist groups targeting critical healthcare infrastructure and the potential for operational paralysis without direct malware deployment.

breaking_news.title: ShinyHunters Steals 1 Petabyte of Data from Telus Digital
breaking_news.summary:
Telecom giant Telus Digital admitted to a massive data breach where the ransomware group ShinyHunters stole up to one petabyte of sensitive information. The stolen data includes support recordings, source code, and employee records, marking one of the largest data thefts in the telecommunications sector.

ShinyHunters has threatened to leak the data if a ransom is not paid, posing significant privacy risks for customers and employees. This breach underscores the vulnerability of outsourcers and telecom providers to sophisticated ransomware operations capable of exfiltrating massive datasets.

breaking_news.title: GlassWorm Supply Chain Attack Compromises Open VSX Extensions
breaking_news.summary:
The GlassWorm threat actor has escalated a supply chain attack by abusing extension dependencies in the Open VSX registry to inject malware into hundreds of Python repositories. Attackers are using stolen GitHub tokens to force-push malicious code into popular projects, including Django apps and ML research code.

This campaign targets developers by posing as helpful tools like linters and formatters, allowing the malware to spread through trusted dependency chains. The compromise of the Open VSX registry represents a significant threat to the software development ecosystem and the integrity of open-source packages.

breaking_news.title: Nine Critical AppArmor Vulnerabilities Expose 12 Million Linux Systems
breaking_news.summary:
Qualys researchers disclosed nine critical vulnerabilities in the Linux AppArmor security module, collectively dubbed CrackArmor, which affect over 12 million enterprise systems. Unprivileged attackers can exploit these flaws to gain full root access, break container isolation, and crash systems without administrative credentials.

Since AppArmor is enabled by default on major distributions like Ubuntu and Debian, the vulnerabilities pose a widespread risk to cloud platforms, IoT devices, and enterprise infrastructure. The flaws have existed since 2017, emphasizing the need for immediate patching across the Linux ecosystem.

breaking_news.title: AWS Bedrock AI Sandbox Flaw Enables Data Exfiltration via DNS
breaking_news.summary:
Researchers discovered a critical flaw in AWS Bedrock AgentCore's Code Interpreter sandbox that allows attackers to bypass isolation boundaries using DNS queries. The vulnerability enables data exfiltration and remote command execution by exploiting the sandbox's permission to make outbound DNS requests.

This finding challenges the security assumptions of agentic AI workflows in cloud environments, as the isolation mechanism failed at the fundamental DNS layer. AWS has acknowledged the report, but the incident highlights the architectural risks inherent in deploying autonomous AI agents with network access.

breaking_news.title: UK Companies House Flaw Exposes Millions of Firm Details
breaking_news.summary:
The UK government agency Companies House confirmed a security flaw in its WebFiling service exposed the personal details of millions of company directors. The vulnerability allowed unauthorized users to view confidential paperwork and alter company records before the service was taken offline for repairs.

This incident compromises the integrity of the UK's corporate registry and exposes sensitive business data to potential misuse. The exposure highlights the critical importance of securing government infrastructure that manages foundational economic data for millions of organizations.

breaking_news.title: LeakNet Ransomware Adopts ClickFix and Deno Runtime for Stealth
breaking_news.summary:
The LeakNet ransomware gang has adopted the ClickFix social engineering tactic, tricking users into manually running malicious commands to gain initial access. The group deploys a malware loader based on the open-source Deno runtime to execute payloads in memory, evading traditional file-based detection.

This shift in tactics demonstrates the evolution of ransomware operations towards stealthier, in-memory techniques that bypass standard security controls. Organizations must update their defenses to detect social engineering attempts and monitor for suspicious runtime activity.

breaking_news.title: RondoDox Botnet Targets 174 Vulnerabilities with 15,000 Daily Attempts
breaking_news.summary:
The RondoDox botnet has expanded its arsenal to target 174 different vulnerabilities, conducting up to 15,000 daily exploitation attempts against various systems. Bitsight reported that the botnet is taking a more focused and strategic approach, mapping 148 CVEs to its active exploitation campaigns.

This widespread activity indicates a coordinated effort to compromise vulnerable infrastructure across the internet, including routers and servers. The high volume of attempts suggests a significant threat to organizations that have not patched known vulnerabilities in their network devices.

breaking_news.title: Intuitive Surgical Discloses Phishing Attack on Internal Systems
breaking_news.summary:
Robotic surgery giant Intuitive Surgical disclosed a cyberattack where unauthorized intruders accessed internal business applications after an employee fell victim to a phishing attack. The company confirmed that while internal IT systems were compromised, its medical devices and hospital networks remain unaffected.

The incident serves as a reminder of the persistent threat of phishing against high-value targets in the healthcare technology sector. Intuitive Surgical is working to secure its environment, but the breach highlights the human element as a primary vector for compromising critical medical infrastructure.

breaking_news.title: North Korean Konni Group Deploys EndRAT via KakaoTalk Phishing
breaking_news.summary:
North Korean threat actors known as Konni have been observed deploying the EndRAT malware through spear-phishing emails targeting KakaoTalk users. The campaign involves compromising victim accounts to distribute malicious payloads to their contacts, leveraging the messaging app for propagation.

This activity demonstrates the group's continued focus on espionage and financial theft through social engineering and messaging platform abuse. Security teams should monitor for suspicious KakaoTalk activity and educate users on the risks of phishing attempts disguised as support messages.
